input.description: Check lunar-claude mise.toml for reference patterns

content.content:
# Mise configuration for terraform-homelab
min_version = "2024.9.5"

[settings]
auto_install = true
not_found_auto_install = true
task_run_auto_install = true

[settings.status]
show_tools = true
show_env = false
truncate = true

[plugins]
fnox-env = "https://github.com/jdx/mise-env-fnox"

[tools]
python = "3.14.3"
eza = "0.23.0"
fd = "10.2.0"
rg = "14.1.1"
git-cliff = "2.10.1"
shellcheck = "0.11.0"
uv = "0.10.4"
ruff = "0.15.2"
# "npm:pyright" = "1.1.407"  # Commented out due to npm registry timeout
lychee = "0.23.0"
"pipx:rumdl" = "0.1.25"
pre-commit = "4.5.1"
fnox = "latest"
age = "latest"

[env]
_.python.venv = { path = ".venv", create = true } # create the venv if it doesn't exist
_.fnox-env = { tools = true }

# === Claude Code CLI ===
[tasks.claude]
description = "Run Claude Code CLI with arguments"
run = "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions"
alias = "ccd"

[tasks.claude-continue]
description = "Run Claude Code CLI with arguments"
run = "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --continue"
alias = "ccdc"

# === Changelog tasks ===
[tasks.changelog]
description = "Update unreleased section of CHANGELOG.md"
shell = "bash -c"
run = """
set -euo pipefail
echo "📝 Updating CHANGELOG.md with unreleased changes..."

# Check if CHANGELOG.md exists
if [ ! -f CHANGELOG.md ]; then
  echo "⚠️  CHANGELOG.md not found. Generating initial changelog..."
  git-cliff -o CHANGELOG.md
  echo "✅ Initial CHANGELOG.md created!"
  exit 0
fi

# Regenerate the entire changelog to include new unreleased commits
git-cliff -o CHANGELOG.md
echo "✅ CHANGELOG.md updated!"
"""

[tasks.changelog-bump]
description = "Bump version and update changelog for a release"
shell = "bash -c"
run = """
set -euo pipefail

if [ -z "${1:-}" ]; then
  echo "❌ Error: Version number required"
  echo ""
  echo "Usage: mise run changelog-bump <version>"
  echo "Example: mise run changelog-bump 0.1.4"
  exit 1
fi

VERSION="$1"

# Validate version format (basic semver check)
if ! echo "$VERSION" | grep -qE '^[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+$'; then
  echo "❌ Error: Invalid version format. Expected: X.Y.Z (e.g., 0.1.4)"
  exit 1
fi

echo "📝 Creating release v$VERSION..."

# Generate changelog for the new version
git-cliff --tag "v$VERSION" -o CHANGELOG.md

echo "✅ CHANGELOG.md updated for v$VERSION!"
echo ""
echo "Next steps:"
echo "  1. Review CHANGELOG.md"
echo "  2. Update version in .claude-plugin/marketplace.json and plugin manifests"
echo "  3. Commit changes:"
echo "     git add CHANGELOG.md .claude-plugin/ && git commit -m 'chore: release v$VERSION'"
echo "  4. Create annotated tag:"
echo "     git tag -a v$VERSION -m 'Release v$VERSION'"
echo "  5. Push to GitHub:"
echo "     git push && git push --tags"
"""

# === Hooks tasks ===
[tasks.hooks-install]
description = "Install pre-commit and infisical hooks"
run = "pre-commit install -f && infisical scan install --pre-commit-hook"

[tasks.pre-commit-run]
description = "Run pre-commit hooks"
run = "pre-commit run --all-files"

# === Infisical tasks ===
[tasks.infisical-scan]
description = "Scan repository for secrets and sensitive data"
run = "infisical scan"

[tasks.markdown-lint]
description = "Lint Markdown files"
run = "rumdl check ."

[tasks.markdown-fix]
description = "Fix Markdown files"
run = "rumdl fmt ."

[tasks.intelligent-lint]
description = "Run intelligent markdown linting with AI agents"
run = "./scripts/intelligent-markdown-lint.py"

[tasks.intelligent-lint-dry-run]
description = "Analyze markdown linting errors (no fixes)"
run = "./scripts/intelligent-markdown-lint.py --dry-run"

[tasks.link-check]
description = "Check all links in markdown files using lychee"
run = "lychee --config lychee.toml '**/*.md'"

[tasks.link-check-verbose]
description = "Check all links in markdown files with verbose output"
run = "lychee --config lychee.toml --verbose debug '**/*.md'"

# === CI/CD Tasks ===
[tasks.ci-lint]
description = "Run all linting checks (used by CI)"
shell = "bash -c"
run = """
set -euo pipefail
echo "Running ruff check..."
uv run ruff check scripts/

echo "Running ruff format check..."
uv run ruff format --check scripts/

echo "Running pyright (warnings allowed)..."
uv run pyright scripts/ || true
"""

[tasks.ci-test]
description = "Run all tests (used by CI)"
shell = "bash -c"
run = """
set -euo pipefail
echo "Running pytest..."
uv run pytest scripts/tests/ -v --tb=short
"""

[tasks.ci-validate]
description = "Validate marketplace structure (used by CI)"
shell = "bash -c"
run = """
set -euo pipefail
echo "Validating marketplace structure..."
uv run scripts/verify-structure.py --strict
"""

[tasks.ci]
description = "Run all CI checks locally"
depends = ["ci-lint", "ci-test", "ci-validate"]

content.text:
Now I have a thorough understanding of the entire ecosystem. Let me compile the complete implementation plan.

---

## Implementation Plan: `preset:base`

### Delivery

Single executable file at `~/.config/mise/tasks/preset/base`. The directory already exists (contains the existing `uv` preset). After creation, run `chmod +x ~/.config/mise/tasks/preset/base`.

### Script Structure Overview

The script follows six sequential phases: preconditions, tool installation, file generation, hk setup, mise tasks and generators, and a summary. Each phase uses functions for isolation, and error handling uses `set -euo pipefail` with a trap for cleanup messaging.

```text
Phase 1: Preconditions     - verify git repo, check existing files
Phase 2: Tool installation - mise use hk git-cliff
Phase 3: File generation   - .gitignore, .editorconfig, cliff.toml, renovate.json
Phase 4: hk setup          - resolve version, write hk.pkl, hk install --mise
Phase 5: mise integration  - tasks add, generate bootstrap, generate github-action
Phase 6: Summary           - print what was created
```

### Complete Script

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#MISE dir="{{cwd}}"
set -euo pipefail

# --- Helpers ----------------------------------------------------------------

info() { printf '\033[0;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$1"; }
warn() { printf '\033[0;33mwarning:\033[0m %s\n' "$1"; }
die()  { printf '\033[0;31merror:\033[0m %s\n' "$1" >&2; exit 1; }

write_file() {
  local dest="$1"
  if [[ -f "$dest" ]]; then
    warn "$dest already exists, overwriting"
  fi
  cat > "$dest"
  info "wrote $dest"
}

# --- Phase 1: Preconditions -------------------------------------------------

if [[ ! -d .git ]]; then
  info "initializing git repository"
  git init
fi

# --- Phase 2: Tool installation ---------------------------------------------

info "installing tools"
mise use hk git-cliff

# --- Phase 3: File generation -----------------------------------------------

info "generating config files"

# .gitignore
write_file .gitignore <<'GITIGNORE'
# === OS ===
.DS_Store
.vscode/
.idea/
*.swp
*.swo
*.log

# === Security ===
*.pem
*.key
*.crt
secrets/
.vault_pass.txt
.env
.env.*.local

# === Claude/AI ===
.specstory/
.entire/
.claude/*.local.md
.claude/convergence-count
.claude/ralph-activity.log
.cursor/
.cursorindexingignore
.mcp.json
GITIGNORE

# .editorconfig
write_file .editorconfig <<'EDITORCONFIG'
root = true

[*]
charset = utf-8
end_of_line = lf
insert_final_newline = true
trim_trailing_whitespace = true
indent_style = space
indent_size = 2

[*.py]
indent_size = 4
max_line_length = 100

[*.md]
trim_trailing_whitespace = false
max_line_length = off

[{Makefile,*.mk}]
indent_style = tab
indent_size = 4
EDITORCONFIG

# cliff.toml
write_file cliff.toml <<'CLIFF'
# git-cliff ~ configuration file
# https://git-cliff.org/docs/configuration


[changelog]
body = """
{% if version %}\
    ## [{{ version | trim_start_matches(pat="v") }}] - {{ timestamp | date(format="%Y-%m-%d") }}
{% else %}\
    ## [unreleased]
{% endif %}\
{% for group, commits in commits | group_by(attribute="group") %}
    ### {{ group | striptags | trim | upper_first }}
    {% for commit in commits %}
        - {% if commit.scope %}*({{ commit.scope }})* {% endif %}\
            {% if commit.breaking %}[**breaking**] {% endif %}\
            {{ commit.message | upper_first }}\
    {% endfor %}
{% endfor %}
"""
trim = true
render_always = true
postprocessors = [
    #{ pattern = '<REPO>', replace = "https://github.com/OWNER/REPO" },
]

[git]
conventional_commits = true
filter_unconventional = true
require_conventional = false
split_commits = false
commit_preprocessors = [
    #{ pattern = '\((\w+\s)?#([0-9]+)\)', replace = "([#${2}](<REPO>/issues/${2}))"},
]
protect_breaking_commits = false
commit_parsers = [
    { message = "^feat", group = "<!-- 0 -->🚀 Features" },
    { message = "^fix", group = "<!-- 1 -->🐛 Bug Fixes" },
    { message = "^doc", group = "<!-- 3 -->📚 Documentation" },
    { message = "^perf", group = "<!-- 4 -->⚡ Performance" },
    { message = "^refactor", group = "<!-- 2 -->🚜 Refactor" },
    { message = "^style", group = "<!-- 5 -->🎨 Styling" },
    { message = "^test", group = "<!-- 6 -->🧪 Testing" },
    { message = "^chore\\(release\\): prepare for", skip = true },
    { message = "^chore\\(deps.*\\)", skip = true },
    { message = "^chore\\(pr\\)", skip = true },
    { message = "^chore\\(pull\\)", skip = true },
    { message = "^chore|^ci", group = "<!-- 7 -->⚙️ Miscellaneous Tasks" },
    { body = ".*security", group = "<!-- 8 -->🛡️ Security" },
    { message = "^revert", group = "<!-- 9 -->◀️ Revert" },
    { message = ".*", group = "<!-- 10 -->💼 Other" },
]
filter_commits = false
link_parsers = []
use_branch_tags = false
topo_order = false
topo_order_commits = true
sort_commits = "oldest"
recurse_submodules = false
CLIFF

# renovate.json
write_file renovate.json <<'RENOVATE'
{
  "$schema": "https://docs.renovatebot.com/renovate-schema.json",
  "extends": ["local>basher83/renovate-config"]
}
RENOVATE

# --- Phase 4: hk setup ------------------------------------------------------

info "configuring hk"

# Resolve installed hk version for PKL package URLs
HK_VERSION=$(mise ls hk --json | python3 -c "
import json, sys
data = json.load(sys.stdin)
versions = data.get('hk', [])
active = [v for v in versions if v.get('active')]
if active:
    print(active[0]['version'])
elif versions:
    print(versions[0]['version'])
else:
    sys.exit(1)
") || die "could not resolve hk version"

info "resolved hk v${HK_VERSION}"

write_file hk.pkl <<HK_PKL
amends "package://github.com/jdx/hk/releases/download/v${HK_VERSION}/hk@${HK_VERSION}#/Config.pkl"
import "package://github.com/jdx/hk/releases/download/v${HK_VERSION}/hk@${HK_VERSION}#/Builtins.pkl"

local base_steps = new Mapping<String, Step> {
    ["trailing_whitespace"] = Builtins.trailing_whitespace
    ["newlines"] = Builtins.newlines
    ["check_merge_conflict"] = Builtins.check_merge_conflict
    ["detect_private_key"] = Builtins.detect_private_key
}

local commit_steps = new Mapping<String, Step> {
    ["check_conventional_commit"] = Builtins.check_conventional_commit
}

hooks {
    ["pre-commit"] {
        fix = true
        stash = "git"
        steps = base_steps
    }
    ["commit-msg"] {
        steps = commit_steps
    }
}
HK_PKL

hk install --mise

# --- Phase 5: mise integration ----------------------------------------------

info "adding mise tasks"

mise tasks add --description "Run all checks" lint -- hk check
mise tasks add --description "Auto-fix issues" fix -- hk fix
mise tasks add --description "Update changelog" changelog -- git-cliff -o CHANGELOG.md
mise tasks add --description "Full CI gate" --depends lint ci -- echo "CI passed"

info "generating mise files"
mise generate bootstrap --localize --write
mise generate github-action --write --task=ci

# --- Phase 6: Summary -------------------------------------------------------

info "preset:base complete"
printf '\n  Files created:\n'
printf '    .gitignore\n'
printf '    .editorconfig\n'
printf '    cliff.toml\n'
printf '    renovate.json\n'
printf '    hk.pkl\n'
printf '    .github/workflows/ci.yml\n'
printf '    bin/mise (bootstrap)\n'
printf '\n  Tools installed:\n'
printf '    hk, git-cliff\n'
printf '\n  Tasks added:\n'
printf '    lint, fix, changelog, ci\n'
printf '\n  Next: run a language preset (e.g., mise preset:uv)\n'
```

### Design Decisions and Rationale

**hk version resolution.** The script runs `mise use hk` first, which installs the tool and pins it in the local `mise.toml`. Then it extracts the exact version from `mise ls hk --json` using a small Python one-liner. Python is always available (mise global config pins python 3.13, and we just ran `mise use` which triggers tool activation). The version gets interpolated into the hk.pkl heredoc (unquoted heredoc delimiter `HK_PKL` allows variable expansion, while all other heredocs use quoted delimiters to prevent expansion of PKL/TOML special characters).

The alternative of parsing `hk --version` output was considered but rejected. That command may print extra text (like "hk version 1.36.0") requiring regex parsing, whereas the JSON from `mise ls` gives a clean structured value. The Python snippet handles two cases: an "active" version (the one pinned in the current directory's mise.toml) or falling back to the first installed version.

**check_conventional_commit placement.** This step is separated into the `commit-msg` hook rather than `pre-commit`. Conventional commit validation operates on the commit message, not on staged files. Placing it in `pre-commit` would mean it runs against file content where it has nothing to do. The `commit-msg` hook is the correct git hook event for message validation.

**hk.pkl using `local` variables.** The base steps and commit steps are defined as `local` PKL variables rather than inlined. This documents intent (base_steps is a named concept) and makes it easier for language presets to understand what the base provides when they write their own complete hk.pkl. Language presets will duplicate the base step references but can reference this file as the canonical list.

**Unquoted vs quoted heredoc.** Only the `hk.pkl` heredoc uses an unquoted delimiter (`<<HK_PKL`) because it needs `${HK_VERSION}` expansion. Every other heredoc uses a quoted delimiter (`<<'CLIFF'`, `<<'GITIGNORE'`, etc.) to prevent the shell from interpreting `$`, backticks, or other special characters that appear in TOML/JSON/editorconfig content.

**Idempotency approach.** The `write_file` helper warns when overwriting an existing file but proceeds. This is intentional: `preset:base` is an opinionated scaffolding tool, not a merge tool. If someone has customized their `.editorconfig`, the warning tells them it was overwritten. The idempotent operations are `mise use` (safe to re-run), `mise tasks add` (overwrites existing task definitions), `mise generate bootstrap --write` (overwrites the bootstrap script), and `mise generate github-action --write` (overwrites the workflow file). No operation leaves partial state on failure because of `set -euo pipefail`.

**No `--pin` on `mise use`.** The command `mise use hk git-cliff` uses fuzzy pinning by default (e.g., writes `hk = "1.36"` to mise.toml). This is correct for most repos where minor version updates should flow in via `mise up --bump`. The exact version is only needed for the PKL URL, which is why we read it from `mise ls --json` separately.

**.editorconfig simplification.** The Virgo-Core template has 157 lines with many redundant sections. Since the default `[*]` block sets `indent_style = space` and `indent_size = 2`, files like `*.yml`, `*.json`, `*.toml`, `*.sh`, `.gitattributes`, `.editorconfig`, `.pre-commit-config.yaml`, `ansible.cfg`, etc. all inherit those defaults. The simplified version keeps only sections that deviate from the default: Python (4-space, max_line_length=100), Markdown (no trim whitespace, no line length), and Makefiles (tab indent). This drops from 157 lines to 18 lines while covering the same behavior for all file types that use 2-space indentation.

**Git repo precondition.** `hk install --mise` writes git hooks, which requires a `.git/` directory. The script checks for `.git/` and runs `git init` if missing. This handles the case where someone creates a new directory, `cd`s into it, and runs `mise preset:base` before initializing git.

### Resulting Directory After Running `mise preset:base`

```text
my-repo/
├── .git/                        # git repo (pre-existing or created)
│   └── hooks/                   # populated by hk install --mise
├── .github/
│   └── workflows/
│       └── ci.yml               # generated by mise generate github-action
├── bin/
│   └── mise                     # generated by mise generate bootstrap --localize
├── .mise/                       # localized mise data (from --localize)
├── .editorconfig                # generated
├── .gitignore                   # generated
├── cliff.toml                   # generated
├── hk.pkl                       # generated (version-pinned)
├── mise.toml                    # created/updated by mise use + mise tasks add
└── renovate.json                # generated
```

The `mise.toml` will contain something like:

```toml
[tools]
hk = "1.36"
git-cliff = "2.10"

[tasks.lint]
description = "Run all checks"
run = "hk check"

[tasks.fix]
description = "Auto-fix issues"
run = "hk fix"

[tasks.changelog]
description = "Update changelog"
run = "git-cliff -o CHANGELOG.md"

[tasks.ci]
description = "Full CI gate"
depends = ["lint"]
run = "echo 'CI passed'"
```

### Edge Cases

**No Python available for version parsing.** The Python one-liner used to parse `mise ls --json` requires Python. This is safe because the global mise config at `~/.config/mise/config.toml` pins `python = "3.13"`, so Python is always available in Brent's environment. If this preset were distributed to others without a global Python, it would need a `jq` fallback or pure-bash JSON parsing. For a personal toolchain preset, Python is the correct choice.

**mise.toml already exists with conflicting tools.** `mise use hk git-cliff` appends to or updates the `[tools]` section. If `mise.toml` already lists an older version of either tool, `mise use` updates the version in place. No conflict.

**hk.pkl already exists from a previous run.** The `write_file` helper warns and overwrites. Since base always writes the same content (modulo hk version), this is safe.

**Network failure during `mise use`.** `mise use` downloads tools from the registry. If the network is down, `mise use` fails, and `set -e` causes the script to exit immediately with a clear error from mise. No partial state.

**Running from a subdirectory.** The `#MISE dir="{{cwd}}"` directive ensures the script runs in the caller's working directory, not the preset script's location (`~/.config/mise/tasks/preset/`). All relative paths in the script (like `.gitignore`, `mise.toml`) resolve correctly.

**Language presets overwriting hk.pkl.** This is by design. The hk reference doc explicitly states that PKL cannot be incrementally modified from bash, so each language preset writes its own complete hk.pkl that includes the base steps plus language-specific ones. The base preset's hk.pkl is a starting point that gets replaced when a language preset runs.

### What the Existing `preset:uv` Needs

The current `preset:uv` at `~/.config/mise/tasks/preset/uv` is stale -- it still uses `pre-commit` instead of `hk`. Once `preset:base` is in place, `preset:uv` should be updated to:
1. Add `#MISE depends=["preset:base"]`
2. Remove `mise use pre-commit` and the pre-commit lint task
3. Add `mise use python uv ruff` instead
4. Write its own hk.pkl that extends the base steps with `ruff` and `ruff_format`

That update is outside the scope of this plan but is the natural next step.

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `~/.config/mise/tasks/preset/base` - The file to create (the entire deliverable)
- `~/.config/mise/tasks/preset/uv` - Existing preset to understand conventions and will need updating afterward
- `/Users/basher8383/3I/workshop/lunar-claude/cliff.toml` - Canonical cliff.toml template to embed verbatim
- `/Users/basher8383/3I/workshop/lunar-claude/plugins/devops/repo-forge/skills/mise/references/hk.md` - hk.pkl structure and builtins reference for the PKL file
- `/Users/basher8383/3I/workshop/lunar-claude/plugins/devops/repo-forge/skills/mise/references/presets.md` - Preset anatomy and API reference for script metadata conventions

---

## Implementation Plan: `preset:base`

### Delivery

Single executable file at `~/.config/mise/tasks/preset/base`. The directory already exists (contains the existing `uv` preset). After creation, run `chmod +x ~/.config/mise/tasks/preset/base`.

### Script Structure Overview

The script follows six sequential phases: preconditions, tool installation, file generation, hk setup, mise tasks and generators, and a summary. Each phase uses functions for isolation, and error handling uses `set -euo pipefail` with a trap for cleanup messaging.

```text
Phase 1: Preconditions     - verify git repo, check existing files
Phase 2: Tool installation - mise use hk git-cliff
Phase 3: File generation   - .gitignore, .editorconfig, cliff.toml, renovate.json
Phase 4: hk setup          - resolve version, write hk.pkl, hk install --mise
Phase 5: mise integration  - tasks add, generate bootstrap, generate github-action

```

### Complete Script

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#MISE dir="{{cwd}}"
set -euo pipefail

# --- Helpers ----------------------------------------------------------------

info() { printf '\033[0;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$1"; }
warn() { printf '\033[0;33mwarning:\033[0m %s\n' "$1"; }
die()  { printf '\033[0;31merror:\033[0m %s\n' "$1" >&2; exit 1; }

write_file() {
  local dest="$1"
  if [[ -f "$dest" ]]; then
    warn "$dest already exists, overwriting"
  fi
  cat > "$dest"
  info "wrote $dest"
}

# --- Phase 1: Preconditions -------------------------------------------------

if [[ ! -d .git ]]; then
  info "initializing git repository"
  git init
fi

# --- Phase 2: Tool installation ---------------------------------------------

info "installing tools"
mise use hk git-cliff

# --- Phase 3: File generation -----------------------------------------------

info "generating config files"

# .gitignore
write_file .gitignore <<'GITIGNORE'
# === OS ===
.DS_Store
.vscode/
.idea/
*.swp
*.swo
*.log

# === Security ===
*.pem
*.key
*.crt
secrets/
.vault_pass.txt
.env
.env.*.local

# === Claude/AI ===
.specstory/
.entire/
.claude/*.local.md
.claude/convergence-count
.claude/ralph-activity.log
.cursor/
.cursorindexingignore
.mcp.json
GITIGNORE

# .editorconfig
write_file .editorconfig <<'EDITORCONFIG'
root = true

[*]
charset = utf-8
end_of_line = lf
insert_final_newline = true
trim_trailing_whitespace = true
indent_style = space
indent_size = 2

[*.py]
indent_size = 4
max_line_length = 100

[*.md]
trim_trailing_whitespace = false
max_line_length = off

[{Makefile,*.mk}]
indent_style = tab
indent_size = 4
EDITORCONFIG

# cliff.toml
write_file cliff.toml <<'CLIFF'
# git-cliff ~ configuration file
# https://git-cliff.org/docs/configuration


[changelog]
body = """
{% if version %}\
    ## [{{ version | trim_start_matches(pat="v") }}] - {{ timestamp | date(format="%Y-%m-%d") }}
{% else %}\
    ## [unreleased]
{% endif %}\
{% for group, commits in commits | group_by(attribute="group") %}
    ### {{ group | striptags | trim | upper_first }}
    {% for commit in commits %}
        - {% if commit.scope %}*({{ commit.scope }})* {% endif %}\
            {% if commit.breaking %}[**breaking**] {% endif %}\
            {{ commit.message | upper_first }}\
    {% endfor %}
{% endfor %}
"""
trim = true
render_always = true
postprocessors = [
    #{ pattern = '<REPO>', replace = "https://github.com/OWNER/REPO" },
]

[git]
conventional_commits = true
filter_unconventional = true
require_conventional = false
split_commits = false
commit_preprocessors = [
    #{ pattern = '\((\w+\s)?#([0-9]+)\)', replace = "([#${2}](<REPO>/issues/${2}))"},
]
protect_breaking_commits = false
commit_parsers = [
    { message = "^feat", group = "<!-- 0 -->🚀 Features" },
    { message = "^fix", group = "<!-- 1 -->🐛 Bug Fixes" },
    { message = "^doc", group = "<!-- 3 -->📚 Documentation" },
    { message = "^perf", group = "<!-- 4 -->⚡ Performance" },
    { message = "^refactor", group = "<!-- 2 -->🚜 Refactor" },
    { message = "^style", group = "<!-- 5 -->🎨 Styling" },
    { message = "^test", group = "<!-- 6 -->🧪 Testing" },
    { message = "^chore\\(release\\): prepare for", skip = true },
    { message = "^chore\\(deps.*\\)", skip = true },
    { message = "^chore\\(pr\\)", skip = true },
    { message = "^chore\\(pull\\)", skip = true },
    { message = "^chore|^ci", group = "<!-- 7 -->⚙️ Miscellaneous Tasks" },
    { body = ".*security", group = "<!-- 8 -->🛡️ Security" },
    { message = "^revert", group = "<!-- 9 -->◀️ Revert" },
    { message = ".*", group = "<!-- 10 -->💼 Other" },
]
filter_commits = false
link_parsers = []
use_branch_tags = false
topo_order = false
topo_order_commits = true
sort_commits = "oldest"
recurse_submodules = false
CLIFF

# renovate.json
write_file renovate.json <<'RENOVATE'
{
  "$schema": "https://docs.renovatebot.com/renovate-schema.json",
  "extends": ["local>basher83/renovate-config"]
}
RENOVATE

# --- Phase 4: hk setup ------------------------------------------------------

info "configuring hk"

# Resolve installed hk version for PKL package URLs
HK_VERSION=$(mise ls hk --json | python3 -c "
import json, sys
data = json.load(sys.stdin)
versions = data.get('hk', [])
active = [v for v in versions if v.get('active')]
if active:
    print(active[0]['version'])
elif versions:
    print(versions[0]['version'])
else:
    sys.exit(1)
") || die "could not resolve hk version"

info "resolved hk v${HK_VERSION}"

write_file hk.pkl <<HK_PKL
amends "package://github.com/jdx/hk/releases/download/v${HK_VERSION}/hk@${HK_VERSION}#/Config.pkl"
import "package://github.com/jdx/hk/releases/download/v${HK_VERSION}/hk@${HK_VERSION}#/Builtins.pkl"

local base_steps = new Mapping<String, Step> {
    ["trailing_whitespace"] = Builtins.trailing_whitespace
    ["newlines"] = Builtins.newlines
    ["check_merge_conflict"] = Builtins.check_merge_conflict
    ["detect_private_key"] = Builtins.detect_private_key
}

local commit_steps = new Mapping<String, Step> {
    ["check_conventional_commit"] = Builtins.check_conventional_commit
}

hooks {
    ["pre-commit"] {
        fix = true
        stash = "git"
        steps = base_steps
    }
    ["commit-msg"] {
        steps = commit_steps
    }
}
HK_PKL

hk install --mise

# --- Phase 5: mise integration ----------------------------------------------

info "adding mise tasks"

mise tasks add --description "Run all checks" lint -- hk check
mise tasks add --description "Auto-fix issues" fix -- hk fix
mise tasks add --description "Update changelog" changelog -- git-cliff -o CHANGELOG.md
mise tasks add --description "Full CI gate" --depends lint ci -- echo "CI passed"

info "generating mise files"
mise generate bootstrap --localize --write
mise generate github-action --write --task=ci

# --- Phase 6: Summary -------------------------------------------------------

info "preset:base complete"
printf '\n  Files created:\n'
printf '    .gitignore\n'
printf '    .editorconfig\n'
printf '    cliff.toml\n'
printf '    renovate.json\n'
printf '    hk.pkl\n'
printf '    .github/workflows/ci.yml\n'
printf '    bin/mise (bootstrap)\n'
printf '\n  Tools installed:\n'
printf '    hk, git-cliff\n'
printf '\n  Tasks added:\n'
printf '    lint, fix, changelog, ci\n'
printf '\n  Next: run a language preset (e.g., mise preset:uv)\n'
```

### Design Decisions and Rationale

**hk version resolution.** The script runs `mise use hk` first, which installs the tool and pins it in the local `mise.toml`. Then it extracts the exact version from `mise ls hk --json` using a small Python one-liner. Python is always available (mise global config pins python 3.13, and we just ran `mise use` which triggers tool activation). The version gets interpolated into the hk.pkl heredoc (unquoted heredoc delimiter `HK_PKL` allows variable expansion, while all other heredocs use quoted delimiters to prevent expansion of PKL/TOML special characters).

The alternative of parsing `hk --version` output was considered but rejected. That command may print extra text (like "hk version 1.36.0") requiring regex parsing, whereas the JSON from `mise ls` gives a clean structured value. The Python snippet handles two cases: an "active" version (the one pinned in the current directory's mise.toml) or falling back to the first installed version.

**check_conventional_commit placement.** This step is separated into the `commit-msg` hook rather than `pre-commit`. Conventional commit validation operates on the commit message, not on staged files. Placing it in `pre-commit` would mean it runs against file content where it has nothing to do. The `commit-msg` hook is the correct git hook event for message validation.

**hk.pkl using `local` variables.** The base steps and commit steps are defined as `local` PKL variables rather than inlined. This documents intent (base_steps is a named concept) and makes it easier for language presets to understand what the base provides when they write their own complete hk.pkl. Language presets will duplicate the base step references but can reference this file as the canonical list.

**Unquoted vs quoted heredoc.** Only the `hk.pkl` heredoc uses an unquoted delimiter (`<<HK_PKL`) because it needs `${HK_VERSION}` expansion. Every other heredoc uses a quoted delimiter (`<<'CLIFF'`, `<<'GITIGNORE'`, etc.) to prevent the shell from interpreting `$`, backticks, or other special characters that appear in TOML/JSON/editorconfig content.

**Idempotency approach.** The `write_file` helper warns when overwriting an existing file but proceeds. This is intentional: `preset:base` is an opinionated scaffolding tool, not a merge tool. If someone has customized their `.editorconfig`, the warning tells them it was overwritten. The idempotent operations are `mise use` (safe to re-run), `mise tasks add` (overwrites existing task definitions), `mise generate bootstrap --write` (overwrites the bootstrap script), and `mise generate github-action --write` (overwrites the workflow file). No operation leaves partial state on failure because of `set -euo pipefail`.

**No `--pin` on `mise use`.** The command `mise use hk git-cliff` uses fuzzy pinning by default (e.g., writes `hk = "1.36"` to mise.toml). This is correct for most repos where minor version updates should flow in via `mise up --bump`. The exact version is only needed for the PKL URL, which is why we read it from `mise ls --json` separately.

**.editorconfig simplification.** The Virgo-Core template has 157 lines with many redundant sections. Since the default `[*]` block sets `indent_style = space` and `indent_size = 2`, files like `*.yml`, `*.json`, `*.toml`, `*.sh`, `.gitattributes`, `.editorconfig`, `.pre-commit-config.yaml`, `ansible.cfg`, etc. all inherit those defaults. The simplified version keeps only sections that deviate from the default: Python (4-space, max_line_length=100), Markdown (no trim whitespace, no line length), and Makefiles (tab indent). This drops from 157 lines to 18 lines while covering the same behavior for all file types that use 2-space indentation.

**Git repo precondition.** `hk install --mise` writes git hooks, which requires a `.git/` directory. The script checks for `.git/` and runs `git init` if missing. This handles the case where someone creates a new directory, `cd`s into it, and runs `mise preset:base` before initializing git.

### Resulting Directory After Running `mise preset:base`

```text
my-repo/
├── .git/                        # git repo (pre-existing or created)
│   └── hooks/                   # populated by hk install --mise
├── .github/
│   └── workflows/
│       └── ci.yml               # generated by mise generate github-action
├── bin/
│   └── mise                     # generated by mise generate bootstrap --localize
├── .mise/                       # localized mise data (from --localize)
├── .editorconfig                # generated
├── .gitignore                   # generated
├── cliff.toml                   # generated
├── hk.pkl                       # generated (version-pinned)
├── mise.toml                    # created/updated by mise use + mise tasks add
└── renovate.json                # generated
```

The `mise.toml` will contain something like:

```toml
[tools]
hk = "1.36"
git-cliff = "2.10"

[tasks.lint]
description = "Run all checks"
run = "hk check"

[tasks.fix]
description = "Auto-fix issues"
run = "hk fix"

[tasks.changelog]
description = "Update changelog"
run = "git-cliff -o CHANGELOG.md"

[tasks.ci]
description = "Full CI gate"
depends = ["lint"]
run = "echo 'CI passed'"
```

### Edge Cases

**No Python available for version parsing.** The Python one-liner used to parse `mise ls --json` requires Python. This is safe because the global mise config at `~/.config/mise/config.toml` pins `python = "3.13"`, so Python is always available in Brent's environment. If this preset were distributed to others without a global Python, it would need a `jq` fallback or pure-bash JSON parsing. For a personal toolchain preset, Python is the correct choice.

**mise.toml already exists with conflicting tools.** `mise use hk git-cliff` appends to or updates the `[tools]` section. If `mise.toml` already lists an older version of either tool, `mise use` updates the version in place. No conflict.

**hk.pkl already exists from a previous run.** The `write_file` helper warns and overwrites. Since base always writes the same content (modulo hk version), this is safe.

**Network failure during `mise use`.** `mise use` downloads tools from the registry. If the network is down, `mise use` fails, and `set -e` causes the script to exit immediately with a clear error from mise. No partial state.

**Running from a subdirectory.** The `#MISE dir="{{cwd}}"` directive ensures the script runs in the caller's working directory, not the preset script's location (`~/.config/mise/tasks/preset/`). All relative paths in the script (like `.gitignore`, `mise.toml`) resolve correctly.

**Language presets overwriting hk.pkl.** This is by design. The hk reference doc explicitly states that PKL cannot be incrementally modified from bash, so each language preset writes its own complete hk.pkl that includes the base steps plus language-specific ones. The base preset's hk.pkl is a starting point that gets replaced when a language preset runs.

### What the Existing `preset:uv` Needs

The current `preset:uv` at `~/.config/mise/tasks/preset/uv` is stale -- it still uses `pre-commit` instead of `hk`. Once `preset:base` is in place, `preset:uv` should be updated to:
1. Add `#MISE depends=["preset:base"]`
2. Remove `mise use pre-commit` and the pre-commit lint task
3. Add `mise use python uv ruff` instead
4. Write its own hk.pkl that extends the base steps with `ruff` and `ruff_format`

That update is outside the scope of this plan but is the natural next step.

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `~/.config/mise/tasks/preset/base` - The file to create (the entire deliverable)
- `~/.config/mise/tasks/preset/uv` - Existing preset to understand conventions and will need updating afterward
- `/Users/basher8383/3I/workshop/lunar-claude/cliff.toml` - Canonical cliff.toml template to embed verbatim
- `/Users/basher8383/3I/workshop/lunar-claude/plugins/devops/repo-forge/skills/mise/references/hk.md` - hk.pkl structure and builtins reference for the PKL file
- `/Users/basher8383/3I/workshop/lunar-claude/plugins/devops/repo-forge/skills/mise/references/presets.md` - Preset anatomy and API reference for script metadata conventions

input.content:
# Plan: Build preset:base

## Context

Step 2 of the repo-forge plugin plan. The mise skill (step 1) is complete and
documents how presets work. Now we're building the actual universal scaffolding
preset that every new repo gets.

## Deliverable

Single file: `~/.config/mise/tasks/preset/base`

An executable bash script that, when run via `mise preset:base` in a project
directory, scaffolds universal config files, installs tools, sets up git hooks,
and adds standard mise tasks.

## What the Script Does

Six sequential phases:

1. **Preconditions** — verify/create `.git/`, warn about existing files
2. **Tool installation** — `mise use hk git-cliff` (versions resolved live)
3. **File generation** — write via heredocs:
   - `.gitignore` — os-base + security + claude-ai modules
   - `.editorconfig` — simplified from Virgo-Core, line length 100
   - `cliff.toml` — canonical template (verbatim from lunar-claude/cliff.toml)
   - `renovate.json` — extends `local>basher83/renovate-config`
4. **hk setup** — resolve installed hk version, write `hk.pkl` with base steps,
   run `hk install --mise`
5. **mise integration** — add tasks (lint, fix, changelog, ci), run
   `mise generate bootstrap --localize --write` and
   `mise generate github-action --write --task=ci`
6. **Summary** — print what was created

## Key Design Decisions

**hk.pkl version pinning**: PKL requires a specific version in package URLs.
After `mise use hk`, resolve the exact version from `hk --version` output and
interpolate into the heredoc. The hk.pkl heredoc is unquoted to allow
`${HK_VERSION}` expansion; all other heredocs are quoted.

**check_conventional_commit in commit-msg hook**: Commit message validation
belongs in the `commit-msg` git hook, not `pre-commit`. Base steps in
`pre-commit`: trailing_whitespace, newlines, check_merge_conflict,
detect_private_key.

**.editorconfig simplification**: Default `[*]` block handles 2-space indent
for everything. Only override sections: Python (4-space, max_line_length=100),
Markdown (no trim, no line length), Makefiles (tabs). 18 lines vs Virgo-Core's
157.

**Idempotency**: `write_file` helper warns on overwrite but proceeds. `mise use`
and `mise tasks add` are naturally idempotent. `set -euo pipefail` prevents
partial state on failure.

**Language presets extending hk.pkl**: PKL can't be incrementally modified from
bash. Each language preset writes its own complete hk.pkl including base steps.
5 lines of duplication per preset — acceptable.

## Resulting Directory

```text
my-repo/
├── .git/hooks/          # populated by hk install --mise
├── .github/workflows/
│   └── ci.yml           # mise generate github-action
├── bin/
│   └── mise             # mise generate bootstrap --localize
├── .editorconfig
├── .gitignore
├── cliff.toml
├── hk.pkl
├── mise.toml            # tools + tasks written by mise use/tasks add
└── renovate.json
```

## Reference Files

- `~/3I/workshop/lunar-claude/cliff.toml` — canonical cliff.toml to embed
- `~/3I/lab/Virgo-Core/.editorconfig` — editorconfig reference (simplified)
- `~/.config/mise/tasks/preset/uv` — existing preset for conventions

## Verification

After writing the script:

1. Create a temp directory, `cd` into it, run `mise preset:base`
2. Verify all files exist with expected content
3. Verify `mise.toml` has hk + git-cliff in tools, lint/fix/changelog/ci tasks
4. Verify `hk.pkl` has correct version and base steps
5. Verify `hk check` runs without errors (on an empty repo, should pass)
6. Verify `mise run lint` works (delegates to `hk check`)

# Plan: Build preset:base

## Context

Step 2 of the repo-forge plugin plan. The mise skill (step 1) is complete and
documents how presets work. Now we're building the actual universal scaffolding
preset that every new repo gets.

## Deliverable

Single file: `~/.config/mise/tasks/preset/base`

An executable bash script that, when run via `mise preset:base` in a project
directory, scaffolds universal config files, installs tools, sets up git hooks,
and adds standard mise tasks.

## What the Script Does

Six sequential phases:

1. **Preconditions** — verify/create `.git/`, warn about existing files
2. **Tool installation** — `mise use hk git-cliff` (versions resolved live)
3. **File generation** — write via heredocs:
   - `.gitignore` — os-base + security + claude-ai modules
   - `.editorconfig` — simplified from Virgo-Core, line length 100
   - `cliff.toml` — canonical template (verbatim from lunar-claude/cliff.toml)
   - `renovate.json` — extends `local>basher83/renovate-config`
4. **hk setup** — resolve installed hk version, write `hk.pkl` with base steps,
   run `hk install --mise`
5. **mise integration** — add tasks (lint, fix, changelog, ci), run
   `mise generate bootstrap --localize --write` and
   `mise generate github-action --write --task=ci`
6. **Summary** — print what was created

## Key Design Decisions

**hk.pkl version pinning**: PKL requires a specific version in package URLs.
After `mise use hk`, resolve the exact version from `hk --version` output and
interpolate into the heredoc. The hk.pkl heredoc is unquoted to allow
`${HK_VERSION}` expansion; all other heredocs are quoted.

**check_conventional_commit in commit-msg hook**: Commit message validation
belongs in the `commit-msg` git hook, not `pre-commit`. Base steps in
`pre-commit`: trailing_whitespace, newlines, check_merge_conflict,
detect_private_key.

**.editorconfig simplification**: Default `[*]` block handles 2-space indent
for everything. Only override sections: Python (4-space, max_line_length=100),
Markdown (no trim, no line length), Makefiles (tabs). 18 lines vs Virgo-Core's
157.

**Idempotency**: `write_file` helper warns on overwrite but proceeds. `mise use`
and `mise tasks add` are naturally idempotent. `set -euo pipefail` prevents
partial state on failure.

**Language presets extending hk.pkl**: PKL can't be incrementally modified from
bash. Each language preset writes its own complete hk.pkl including base steps.
5 lines of duplication per preset — acceptable.

## Resulting Directory

```text
my-repo/
├── .git/hooks/          # populated by hk install --mise
├── .github/workflows/
│   └── ci.yml           # mise generate github-action
├── bin/
│   └── mise             # mise generate bootstrap --localize
├── .editorconfig
├── .gitignore
├── cliff.toml
├── hk.pkl
├── mise.toml            # tools + tasks written by mise use/tasks add
└── renovate.json
```

## Reference Files

- `~/3I/workshop/lunar-claude/cliff.toml` — canonical cliff.toml to embed
- `~/3I/lab/Virgo-Core/.editorconfig` — editorconfig reference (simplified)
- `~/.config/mise/tasks/preset/uv` — existing preset for conventions

## Verification

After writing the script:

1. Create a temp directory, `cd` into it, run `mise preset:base`
2. Verify all files exist with expected content
3. Verify `mise.toml` has hk + git-cliff in tools, lint/fix/changelog/ci tasks
4. Verify `hk.pkl` has correct version and base steps
5. Verify `hk check` runs without errors (on an empty repo, should pass)
6. Verify `mise run lint` works (delegates to `hk check`)

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#MISE dir="{{cwd}}"
set -euo pipefail

# --- Helpers ----------------------------------------------------------------

info() { printf '\033[0;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$1"; }
warn() { printf '\033[0;33mwarning:\033[0m %s\n' "$1"; }
die()  { printf '\033[0;31merror:\033[0m %s\n' "$1" >&2; exit 1; }

write_file() {
  local dest="$1"
  if [[ -f "$dest" ]]; then
    warn "$dest already exists, overwriting"
  fi
  cat > "$dest"
  info "wrote $dest"
}

# --- Phase 1: Preconditions -------------------------------------------------

if [[ ! -d .git ]]; then
  info "initializing git repository"
  git init
fi

# --- Phase 2: Tool installation ---------------------------------------------

info "installing tools"
mise use hk git-cliff

# --- Phase 3: File generation -----------------------------------------------

info "generating config files"

write_file .gitignore <<'GITIGNORE'
# === OS ===
.DS_Store
.vscode/
.idea/
*.swp
*.swo
*.log

# === Security ===
*.pem
*.key
*.crt
secrets/
.vault_pass.txt
.env
.env.*.local

# === Claude/AI ===
.specstory/
.entire/
.claude/*.local.md
.claude/convergence-count
.claude/ralph-activity.log
.cursor/
.cursorindexingignore
.mcp.json
GITIGNORE

write_file .editorconfig <<'EDITORCONFIG'
root = true

[*]
charset = utf-8
end_of_line = lf
insert_final_newline = true
trim_trailing_whitespace = true
indent_style = space
indent_size = 2

[*.py]
indent_size = 4
max_line_length = 100

[*.md]
trim_trailing_whitespace = false
max_line_length = off

[{Makefile,*.mk}]
indent_style = tab
indent_size = 4
EDITORCONFIG

write_file cliff.toml <<'CLIFF'
# git-cliff ~ configuration file
# https://git-cliff.org/docs/configuration


[changelog]
body = """
{% if version %}\
    ## [{{ version | trim_start_matches(pat="v") }}] - {{ timestamp | date(format="%Y-%m-%d") }}
{% else %}\
    ## [unreleased]
{% endif %}\
{% for group, commits in commits | group_by(attribute="group") %}
    ### {{ group | striptags | trim | upper_first }}
    {% for commit in commits %}
        - {% if commit.scope %}*({{ commit.scope }})* {% endif %}\
            {% if commit.breaking %}[**breaking**] {% endif %}\
            {{ commit.message | upper_first }}\
    {% endfor %}
{% endfor %}
"""
trim = true
render_always = true
postprocessors = [
    #{ pattern = '<REPO>', replace = "https://github.com/OWNER/REPO" },
]

[git]
conventional_commits = true
filter_unconventional = true
require_conventional = false
split_commits = false
commit_preprocessors = [
    #{ pattern = '\((\w+\s)?#([0-9]+)\)', replace = "([#${2}](<REPO>/issues/${2}))"},
]
protect_breaking_commits = false
commit_parsers = [
    { message = "^feat", group = "<!-- 0 -->🚀 Features" },
    { message = "^fix", group = "<!-- 1 -->🐛 Bug Fixes" },
    { message = "^doc", group = "<!-- 3 -->📚 Documentation" },
    { message = "^perf", group = "<!-- 4 -->⚡ Performance" },
    { message = "^refactor", group = "<!-- 2 -->🚜 Refactor" },
    { message = "^style", group = "<!-- 5 -->🎨 Styling" },
    { message = "^test", group = "<!-- 6 -->🧪 Testing" },
    { message = "^chore\\(release\\): prepare for", skip = true },
    { message = "^chore\\(deps.*\\)", skip = true },
    { message = "^chore\\(pr\\)", skip = true },
    { message = "^chore\\(pull\\)", skip = true },
    { message = "^chore|^ci", group = "<!-- 7 -->⚙️ Miscellaneous Tasks" },
    { body = ".*security", group = "<!-- 8 -->🛡️ Security" },
    { message = "^revert", group = "<!-- 9 -->◀️ Revert" },
    { message = ".*", group = "<!-- 10 -->💼 Other" },
]
filter_commits = false
link_parsers = []
use_branch_tags = false
topo_order = false
topo_order_commits = true
sort_commits = "oldest"
recurse_submodules = false
CLIFF

write_file renovate.json <<'RENOVATE'
{
  "$schema": "https://docs.renovatebot.com/renovate-schema.json",
  "extends": ["local>basher83/renovate-config"]
}
RENOVATE

# --- Phase 4: hk setup ------------------------------------------------------

info "configuring hk"

HK_VERSION=$(hk --version | grep -oE '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1) \
  || die "could not resolve hk version"

info "resolved hk v${HK_VERSION}"

write_file hk.pkl <<HK_PKL
amends "package://github.com/jdx/hk/releases/download/v${HK_VERSION}/hk@${HK_VERSION}#/Config.pkl"
import "package://github.com/jdx/hk/releases/download/v${HK_VERSION}/hk@${HK_VERSION}#/Builtins.pkl"

local base_steps = new Mapping<String, Step> {
    ["trailing_whitespace"] = Builtins.trailing_whitespace
    ["newlines"] = Builtins.newlines
    ["check_merge_conflict"] = Builtins.check_merge_conflict
    ["detect_private_key"] = Builtins.detect_private_key
}

local commit_steps = new Mapping<String, Step> {
    ["check_conventional_commit"] = Builtins.check_conventional_commit
}

hooks {
    ["pre-commit"] {
        fix = true
        stash = "git"
        steps = base_steps
    }
    ["commit-msg"] {
        steps = commit_steps
    }
}
HK_PKL

hk install --mise

# --- Phase 5: mise integration ----------------------------------------------

info "adding mise tasks"

mise tasks add --description "Run all checks" lint -- hk check
mise tasks add --description "Auto-fix issues" fix -- hk fix
mise tasks add --description "Update changelog" changelog -- git-cliff -o CHANGELOG.md
mise tasks add --description "Full CI gate" --depends lint ci -- echo "CI passed"

info "generating mise scaffolding"
mise generate bootstrap --localize --write
mise generate github-action --write --task=ci

# --- Phase 6: Summary -------------------------------------------------------

info "preset:base complete"
printf '\n  Files created:\n'
printf '    .gitignore\n'
printf '    .editorconfig\n'
printf '    cliff.toml\n'
printf '    renovate.json\n'
printf '    hk.pkl\n'
printf '    .github/workflows/ci.yml\n'
printf '    bin/mise (bootstrap)\n'
printf '\n  Tools pinned:\n'
printf '    hk, git-cliff\n'
printf '\n  Tasks added:\n'
printf '    lint, fix, changelog, ci\n'
printf '\n  Next: run a language preset (e.g., mise preset:uv)\n'

data.command: jq -r '.tool_input.file_path' | while read file_path; do if [[ "$file_path" =~ \.py$ ]]; then ruff check --fix "$file_path" && ruff format "$file_path"; fi; done

    ["check_conventional_commit"] = Builtins.check_conventional_commit
}

hooks {
    ["pre-commit"] {
        fix = true
        stash = "git"
        steps = base_steps
    }
    ["commit-msg"] {
        steps = commit_steps
    }
}

info "generating mise scaffolding"
mise generate bootstrap --localize --write
mise generate github-action --write --task=ci

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#MISE dir="{{cwd}}"
set -euo pipefail

# --- Helpers ----------------------------------------------------------------

info() { printf '\033[0;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$1"; }
warn() { printf '\033[0;33mwarning:\033[0m %s\n' "$1"; }
die()  { printf '\033[0;31merror:\033[0m %s\n' "$1" >&2; exit 1; }

write_file() {
  local dest="$1"
  if [[ -f "$dest" ]]; then
    warn "$dest already exists, overwriting"
  fi
  cat > "$dest"
  info "wrote $dest"
}

# --- Phase 1: Preconditions -------------------------------------------------

if [[ ! -d .git ]]; then
  info "initializing git repository"
  git init
fi

# --- Phase 2: Tool installation ---------------------------------------------

info "installing tools"
mise use hk git-cliff

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#MISE dir="{{cwd}}"
set -euo pipefail

# --- Helpers ----------------------------------------------------------------

info() { printf '\033[0;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$1"; }
warn() { printf '\033[0;33mwarning:\033[0m %s\n' "$1"; }
die()  { printf '\033[0;31merror:\033[0m %s\n' "$1" >&2; exit 1; }

write_file() {
  local dest="$1"
  if [[ -f "$dest" ]]; then
    warn "$dest already exists, overwriting"
  fi
  cat > "$dest"
  info "wrote $dest"
}

# --- Phase 1: Preconditions -------------------------------------------------

if [[ ! -d .git ]]; then
  info "initializing git repository"
  git init
fi

# --- Phase 2: Tool installation ---------------------------------------------

info "installing tools"
mise use hk git-cliff

# --- Phase 3: File generation -----------------------------------------------

info "generating config files"

write_file .gitignore <<'GITIGNORE'
# === OS ===
.DS_Store
.vscode/
.idea/
*.swp
*.swo
*.log

# === Security ===
*.pem
*.key
*.crt
secrets/
.vault_pass.txt
.env
.env.*.local

# === Claude/AI ===
.specstory/
.entire/
.claude/*.local.md
.claude/convergence-count
.claude/ralph-activity.log
.cursor/
.cursorindexingignore
.mcp.json
GITIGNORE

write_file .editorconfig <<'EDITORCONFIG'
root = true

[*]
charset = utf-8
end_of_line = lf
insert_final_newline = true
trim_trailing_whitespace = true
indent_style = space
indent_size = 2

[*.py]
indent_size = 4
max_line_length = 100

[*.md]
trim_trailing_whitespace = false
max_line_length = off

[{Makefile,*.mk}]
indent_style = tab
indent_size = 4
EDITORCONFIG

write_file cliff.toml <<'CLIFF'
# git-cliff ~ configuration file
# https://git-cliff.org/docs/configuration


[changelog]
body = """
{% if version %}\
    ## [{{ version | trim_start_matches(pat="v") }}] - {{ timestamp | date(format="%Y-%m-%d") }}
{% else %}\
    ## [unreleased]
{% endif %}\
{% for group, commits in commits | group_by(attribute="group") %}
    ### {{ group | striptags | trim | upper_first }}
    {% for commit in commits %}
        - {% if commit.scope %}*({{ commit.scope }})* {% endif %}\
            {% if commit.breaking %}[**breaking**] {% endif %}\
            {{ commit.message | upper_first }}\
    {% endfor %}
{% endfor %}
"""
trim = true
render_always = true
postprocessors = [
    #{ pattern = '<REPO>', replace = "https://github.com/OWNER/REPO" },
]

[git]
conventional_commits = true
filter_unconventional = true
require_conventional = false
split_commits = false
commit_preprocessors = [
    #{ pattern = '\((\w+\s)?#([0-9]+)\)', replace = "([#${2}](<REPO>/issues/${2}))"},
]
protect_breaking_commits = false
commit_parsers = [
    { message = "^feat", group = "<!-- 0 -->🚀 Features" },
    { message = "^fix", group = "<!-- 1 -->🐛 Bug Fixes" },
    { message = "^doc", group = "<!-- 3 -->📚 Documentation" },
    { message = "^perf", group = "<!-- 4 -->⚡ Performance" },
    { message = "^refactor", group = "<!-- 2 -->🚜 Refactor" },
    { message = "^style", group = "<!-- 5 -->🎨 Styling" },
    { message = "^test", group = "<!-- 6 -->🧪 Testing" },
    { message = "^chore\\(release\\): prepare for", skip = true },
    { message = "^chore\\(deps.*\\)", skip = true },
    { message = "^chore\\(pr\\)", skip = true },
    { message = "^chore\\(pull\\)", skip = true },
    { message = "^chore|^ci", group = "<!-- 7 -->⚙️ Miscellaneous Tasks" },
    { body = ".*security", group = "<!-- 8 -->🛡️ Security" },
    { message = "^revert", group = "<!-- 9 -->◀️ Revert" },
    { message = ".*", group = "<!-- 10 -->💼 Other" },
]
filter_commits = false
link_parsers = []
use_branch_tags = false
topo_order = false
topo_order_commits = true
sort_commits = "oldest"
recurse_submodules = false
CLIFF

write_file renovate.json <<'RENOVATE'
{
  "$schema": "https://docs.renovatebot.com/renovate-schema.json",
  "extends": ["local>basher83/renovate-config"]
}
RENOVATE

# --- Phase 4: hk setup ------------------------------------------------------

info "configuring hk"

HK_VERSION=$(hk --version | grep -oE '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1) \
  || die "could not resolve hk version"

info "resolved hk v${HK_VERSION}"

write_file hk.pkl <<HK_PKL
amends "package://github.com/jdx/hk/releases/download/v${HK_VERSION}/hk@${HK_VERSION}#/Config.pkl"
import "package://github.com/jdx/hk/releases/download/v${HK_VERSION}/hk@${HK_VERSION}#/Builtins.pkl"

local base_steps = new Mapping<String, Step> {
    ["trailing_whitespace"] = Builtins.trailing_whitespace
    ["newlines"] = Builtins.newlines
    ["check_merge_conflict"] = Builtins.check_merge_conflict
    ["detect_private_key"] = Builtins.detect_private_key
}

local commit_steps = new Mapping<String, Step> {
    ["check_conventional_commit"] = Builtins.check_conventional_commit
}

hooks {
    ["pre-commit"] {
        fix = true
        stash = "git"
        steps = base_steps
    }
    ["commit-msg"] {
        steps = commit_steps
    }
}
HK_PKL

hk install --mise

# --- Phase 5: mise integration ----------------------------------------------

info "adding mise tasks"

mise tasks add --description "Run all checks" lint -- hk check
mise tasks add --description "Auto-fix issues" fix -- hk fix
mise tasks add --description "Update changelog" changelog -- git-cliff -o CHANGELOG.md
mise tasks add --description "Full CI gate" --depends lint ci -- echo "CI passed"

info "generating mise scaffolding"
mise generate bootstrap --localize --write
mise generate github-action --write --task=ci

# --- Phase 6: Summary -------------------------------------------------------

info "preset:base complete"
printf '\n  Files created:\n'
printf '    .gitignore\n'
printf '    .editorconfig\n'
printf '    cliff.toml\n'
printf '    renovate.json\n'
printf '    hk.pkl\n'
printf '    .github/workflows/ci.yml\n'
printf '    bin/mise (bootstrap)\n'
printf '\n  Tools pinned:\n'
printf '    hk, git-cliff\n'
printf '\n  Tasks added:\n'
printf '    lint, fix, changelog, ci\n'
printf '\n  Next: run a language preset (e.g., mise preset:uv)\n'

content.thinking:
Everything looks good. The files are all created correctly. Let me now test `hk check` and `mise run lint` to make sure the hooks work.

One issue I notice: `mise use` wrote `git-cliff = "latest"`, `hk = "latest"`, `pkl = "latest"` instead of the resolved fuzzy versions like `hk = "1.36"`. That's a bit surprising. Let me check the mise version on this system... Actually, `mise use hk` should write the version that was resolved. But it wrote `"latest"`. This might be a mise behavior change, or it could be that when no version is specified, it defaults to `latest` as the version string. 

Looking at the mise documentation, the default behavior is fuzzy pinning — `mise use python@3.13` writes `python = "3.13"` — but you need to specify the version explicitly. Without a version, it defaults to `latest`, which means it will always pull the newest version rather than pinning to what was actually installed. That's a meaningful difference from what I was expecting.

For a scaffold preset, using `latest` might actually be the right call since these are dev tools like hk and git-cliff, not runtime dependencies. The repo would always get the newest versions on `mise install`. But the plan specifically says to use fuzzy pinning by default, so I need to figure out how to reconcile that with not hardcoding versions.

The real issue is that `mise use hk` without specifying a version writes `latest`, whereas `mise use python@3.13` gives you fuzzy pinning. For tooling dependencies, `latest` makes sense, but I should verify `hk check` works and then test the lint command to see if everything's functioning properly.

content.thinking:
`hk check` fails because our hk.pkl only defines `pre-commit` and `commit-msg` hooks, not a standalone `check` hook. Looking at the hk reference:

| Hook | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `pre-commit` | Run before commit creation. Supports fix and stash. |
| `pre-push` | Run before push. Typically check-only. |
| `commit-msg` | Validate commit message format. |
| `prepare-commit-msg` | Template or modify commit messages. |
| `check` | Standalone `hk check` command (not tied to git). |


| `fix` | Standalone `hk fix` command (not tied to git). |

I need to add `check` and `fix` hooks to hk.pkl that mirror the pre-commit steps—the `check` hook should run the same validations as pre-commit without the stash behavior, and `fix` should apply the same fixes.

The key distinction is that `hk check` and `hk fix` are manual commands I can run directly, while the pre-commit hook runs automatically during git commits. So I need to define all three hooks in the config: `pre-commit` for git integration, `check` for the standalone validation command, and `fix` for the standalone fix command.

content.text: Right — `hk check` and `hk fix` are standalone hook types that need their own definitions in hk.pkl, separate from `pre-commit`. Let me fix the template.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#MISE dir="{{cwd}}"
set -euo pipefail

# --- Helpers ----------------------------------------------------------------

info() { printf '\033[0;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$1"; }
warn() { printf '\033[0;33mwarning:\033[0m %s\n' "$1"; }
die()  { printf '\033[0;31merror:\033[0m %s\n' "$1" >&2; exit 1; }

write_file() {
  local dest="$1"
  if [[ -f "$dest" ]]; then
    warn "$dest already exists, overwriting"
  fi
  cat > "$dest"
  info "wrote $dest"
}

# --- Phase 1: Preconditions -------------------------------------------------

if [[ ! -d .git ]]; then
  info "initializing git repository"
  git init
fi

# --- Phase 2: Tool installation ---------------------------------------------

info "installing tools"
mise use hk pkl git-cliff

# --- Phase 3: File generation -----------------------------------------------

info "generating config files"

write_file .gitignore <<'GITIGNORE'
# === OS ===
.DS_Store
.vscode/
.idea/
*.swp
*.swo
*.log

# === Security ===
*.pem
*.key
*.crt
secrets/
.vault_pass.txt
.env
.env.*.local

# === Claude/AI ===
.specstory/
.entire/
.claude/*.local.md
.claude/convergence-count
.claude/ralph-activity.log
.cursor/
.cursorindexingignore
.mcp.json
GITIGNORE

write_file .editorconfig <<'EDITORCONFIG'
root = true

[*]
charset = utf-8
end_of_line = lf
insert_final_newline = true
trim_trailing_whitespace = true
indent_style = space
indent_size = 2

[*.py]
indent_size = 4
max_line_length = 100

[*.md]
trim_trailing_whitespace = false
max_line_length = off

[{Makefile,*.mk}]
indent_style = tab
indent_size = 4
EDITORCONFIG

write_file cliff.toml <<'CLIFF'
# git-cliff ~ configuration file
# https://git-cliff.org/docs/configuration


[changelog]
body = """
{% if version %}\
    ## [{{ version | trim_start_matches(pat="v") }}] - {{ timestamp | date(format="%Y-%m-%d") }}
{% else %}\
    ## [unreleased]
{% endif %}\
{% for group, commits in commits | group_by(attribute="group") %}
    ### {{ group | striptags | trim | upper_first }}
    {% for commit in commits %}
        - {% if commit.scope %}*({{ commit.scope }})* {% endif %}\
            {% if commit.breaking %}[**breaking**] {% endif %}\
            {{ commit.message | upper_first }}\
    {% endfor %}
{% endfor %}
"""
trim = true
render_always = true
postprocessors = [
    #{ pattern = '<REPO>', replace = "https://github.com/OWNER/REPO" },
]

[git]
conventional_commits = true
filter_unconventional = true
require_conventional = false
split_commits = false
commit_preprocessors = [
    #{ pattern = '\((\w+\s)?#([0-9]+)\)', replace = "([#${2}](<REPO>/issues/${2}))"},
]
protect_breaking_commits = false
commit_parsers = [
    { message = "^feat", group = "<!-- 0 -->🚀 Features" },
    { message = "^fix", group = "<!-- 1 -->🐛 Bug Fixes" },
    { message = "^doc", group = "<!-- 3 -->📚 Documentation" },
    { message = "^perf", group = "<!-- 4 -->⚡ Performance" },
    { message = "^refactor", group = "<!-- 2 -->🚜 Refactor" },
    { message = "^style", group = "<!-- 5 -->🎨 Styling" },
    { message = "^test", group = "<!-- 6 -->🧪 Testing" },
    { message = "^chore\\(release\\): prepare for", skip = true },
    { message = "^chore\\(deps.*\\)", skip = true },
    { message = "^chore\\(pr\\)", skip = true },
    { message = "^chore\\(pull\\)", skip = true },
    { message = "^chore|^ci", group = "<!-- 7 -->⚙️ Miscellaneous Tasks" },
    { body = ".*security", group = "<!-- 8 -->🛡️ Security" },
    { message = "^revert", group = "<!-- 9 -->◀️ Revert" },
    { message = ".*", group = "<!-- 10 -->💼 Other" },
]
filter_commits = false
link_parsers = []
use_branch_tags = false
topo_order = false
topo_order_commits = true
sort_commits = "oldest"
recurse_submodules = false
CLIFF

write_file renovate.json <<'RENOVATE'
{
  "$schema": "https://docs.renovatebot.com/renovate-schema.json",
  "extends": ["local>basher83/renovate-config"]
}
RENOVATE

# --- Phase 4: hk setup ------------------------------------------------------

info "configuring hk"

HK_VERSION=$(hk --version | grep -oE '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1) \
  || die "could not resolve hk version"

info "resolved hk v${HK_VERSION}"

write_file hk.pkl <<HK_PKL
amends "package://github.com/jdx/hk/releases/download/v${HK_VERSION}/hk@${HK_VERSION}#/Config.pkl"
import "package://github.com/jdx/hk/releases/download/v${HK_VERSION}/hk@${HK_VERSION}#/Builtins.pkl"

local base_steps = new Mapping<String, Step> {
    ["trailing_whitespace"] = Builtins.trailing_whitespace
    ["newlines"] = Builtins.newlines
    ["check_merge_conflict"] = Builtins.check_merge_conflict
    ["detect_private_key"] = Builtins.detect_private_key
}

local commit_steps = new Mapping<String, Step> {
    ["check_conventional_commit"] = Builtins.check_conventional_commit
}

hooks {
    ["pre-commit"] {
        fix = true
        stash = "git"
        steps = base_steps
    }
    ["commit-msg"] {
        steps = commit_steps
    }
}
HK_PKL

hk install --mise

# --- Phase 5: mise integration ----------------------------------------------

info "adding mise tasks"

mise tasks add --description "Run all checks" lint -- hk check
mise tasks add --description "Auto-fix issues" fix -- hk fix
mise tasks add --description "Update changelog" changelog -- git-cliff -o CHANGELOG.md
mise tasks add --description "Full CI gate" --depends lint ci -- echo "CI passed"

info "generating mise scaffolding"
mise generate bootstrap --localize --write
mise generate github-action --write --task=ci

# --- Phase 6: Summary -------------------------------------------------------

info "preset:base complete"
printf '\n  Files created:\n'
printf '    .gitignore\n'
printf '    .editorconfig\n'
printf '    cliff.toml\n'
printf '    renovate.json\n'
printf '    hk.pkl\n'
printf '    .github/workflows/ci.yml\n'
printf '    bin/mise (bootstrap)\n'
printf '\n  Tools pinned:\n'
printf '    hk, git-cliff\n'
printf '\n  Tasks added:\n'
printf '    lint, fix, changelog, ci\n'
printf '\n  Next: run a language preset (e.g., mise preset:uv)\n'

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# repo-forge — Working Plan

## Status

Plugin scaffolded and registered. Mise skill moved in. Marketplace validated.
Two commits on main ahead of origin.

## Completed

- Plugin structure at plugins/devops/repo-forge/
- plugin.json, README.md
- Mise skill with examples and references (python, rust)
- Removed stale `experimental = true` from mise skill
- Added trigger phrases for env vars and layered config
- Registered in marketplace.json, verify-structure passes clean

## Rethinking Scope

Original plan was 5 separate skills: mise, pre-commit, gitignore, git-cliff,
project-setup. After reviewing config-aggregation.md and mise's preset/hook
capabilities, the architecture simplifies.

The real delivery mechanism is mise presets — executable task scripts that
scaffold repos using `mise use` (real version resolution), `mise tasks add`,
and `mise config set`. The skills teach Claude how to use and compose these
presets. The presets do the actual work.

This could collapse to:

1. **mise skill** (expanded) — covers config, tasks, hooks, presets, `mise
   generate`, and the version resolution pattern. The foundational knowledge
   layer.

2. **repo-onboard command or skill** — the "onboard this repo" directive that
   identifies project type and runs the right preset chain. Consumes everything
   the mise skill teaches.

The other originally planned skills (pre-commit, gitignore, git-cliff) become
reference material inside the mise skill or preset logic rather than standalone
skills. Their configs are generated by presets, not hand-written with skill
guidance.

## Key Design Constraints

- NEVER guess version numbers. Use `mise use <tool>` (pins current version at
  scaffold time by querying the registry), `mise ls-remote <tool>` (list
  available), or `mise ls` (what's installed). Training data versions are stale.
- Presets use `mise use` which resolves and pins at scaffold time.
- Mise hooks (enter, postinstall, watch_files) enable runtime validation,
  not just one-shot scaffolding.

## Data Sources

- ~/3I/config-aggregation.md — Phase 2 deep comparison across 35 repos
- ~/.config/mise/tasks/preset/uv — existing preset (minimal, needs expansion)
- https://mise.jdx.dev/mise-cookbook/presets.html — preset docs
- https://mise.jdx.dev/hooks.html — hook events and config
- https://mise.jdx.dev/cli/generate — mise generate subcommands
- DeepWiki jdx/mise — registry, backends, generate internals
- Context7 /websites/mise_jdx_dev — full docs reference

## Research Findings (DeepWiki + Context7)

### `mise generate` — built-in scaffolding

Mise already has generation capabilities we should leverage instead of
reimplementing:

- `mise generate bootstrap` — creates a script that downloads and installs
  mise for contributors who don't have it. Supports `--localize` to sandbox
  mise into .mise/ within the project. Pair with `mise generate task-stubs`
  to let contributors run tasks without global mise.

- `mise generate config` — creates a mise.toml, can import from existing
  .tool-versions files.

- `mise generate git-pre-commit` — generates a git pre-commit hook that runs
  a mise task (e.g., `mise run pre-commit`). Complements the pre-commit
  framework rather than replacing it. Passes staged files via $STAGED.

- `mise generate github-action` — generates a CI workflow that runs `mise run
  ci` on push. Uses jdx/mise-action.

- `mise generate task-docs` — generates documentation for all defined tasks.
  Can inject into existing files or render per-task docs.

- `mise generate task-stubs` — creates shims in bin/ so contributors can run
  `./bin/<task>` without mise installed. Pairs with bootstrap.

Key insight: presets should call `mise generate` where appropriate rather than
writing files from scratch. For example, preset:base should run
`mise generate bootstrap --localize --write` and
`mise generate git-pre-commit --task=pre-commit --write`.

### `mise use` — version resolution lifecycle

1. Parses TOOL@VERSION argument
2. Selects config file (local mise.toml, global, or --path target)
3. Resolves version: checks lockfile first, then queries backend registry
4. Installs if not present
5. Writes pinned version to mise.toml
6. Rebuilds shims

Default is fuzzy pinning (`mise use python@3.13` writes `python = "3.13"`).
Use `--pin` for exact (`python = "3.13.2"`). This is the correct mechanism
for presets — never hardcode versions.

### `mise config set` — programmatic configuration

Can set arbitrary config paths:
- `mise config set tools.python 3.12`
- `mise config set env._.python.venv.path .venv`
- `mise config set env._.python.venv.create true -t bool`
- `mise config set settings.jobs 4`
- Supports --type flag: string, bool, list, set

### Registry and backends

Mise has a three-layer architecture:
1. Registry layer — maps short names to backends (compiled from TOML files)
2. Backend resolution — converts to concrete installers
3. Installation — download, verify, extract, install

Backends: core (node, python, ruby, go, java), aqua (ideal for new tools,
no plugins needed, works on Windows), cargo, npm, pipx, go, github, gitlab,
asdf (legacy), vfox, dotnet, gem, conda, http, s3.

The aqua registry is compiled into the mise binary — no external lookups
needed for tools registered there.

### Task dependency system

Three dependency types:
- `depends` — must complete before task runs
- `wait_for` — soft dependency, waits only if also being run
- `depends_post` — runs after task completes (success or failure)

Dependency args and env can be passed inline:
```toml
[tasks.deploy]
depends = [
  { task = "build", args = ["--release"],
    env = { RUSTFLAGS = "-C opt-level=3" } }
]
```

Tasks support `sources` and `outputs` for incremental execution — skip if
outputs are newer than sources. Useful for build/compile tasks.

### Hooks

Events: enter (once on project entry), cd (every dir change), leave (exit
project), preinstall, postinstall, watch_files.

Can trigger mise tasks directly:
```toml
[hooks]
enter = { task = "setup" }
```

Shell hooks execute in current shell (can source files):
```toml
[hooks.enter]
shell = "bash"
script = "source completions.sh"
```

watch_files monitors patterns and auto-runs:
```toml
[[watch_files]]
patterns = ["src/**/*.rs"]
run = "cargo fmt"
```

postinstall gets $MISE_INSTALLED_TOOLS (JSON array) and per-tool
$MISE_TOOL_NAME, $MISE_TOOL_VERSION, $MISE_TOOL_INSTALL_PATH.

### `hk` — pre-commit replacement (jdx/hk)

hk is a Rust-based git hook manager by the same author as mise. It replaces
the pre-commit framework entirely with significant advantages:

Architecture:
- Read/write file locks enable aggressive parallelism without race conditions
- `check` steps get read locks (run concurrently), `fix` steps get write locks
- `check_first = true` (default): runs check with read lock first, only
  acquires write lock if check fails. Optimizes the common case.
- Uses libgit2 directly instead of shelling out to git (faster)
- Stashes unstaged changes before fix hooks (solves the pre-commit staged/
  unstaged mixing bug)

Configuration: hk.pkl (Apple's PKL language)
- Typed configuration with schema validation
- Builtins for common tools (no version pinning of hook repos needed)
- Also supports TOML/YAML/JSON but builtins only available in PKL

Builtins (built-in linter/formatter definitions):
- JS/TS: prettier, eslint, tsc, biome, deno
- Python: black, ruff, mypy, pylint
- Rust: cargo_fmt, cargo_clippy, rustfmt
- Go: go_fmt, go_imports, golangci_lint, go_sec
- Ruby: rubocop, standard_rb
- Shell: shellcheck, shfmt
- Config: yamllint, yamlfmt, taplo (TOML), jq (JSON), pkl
- Markup: markdown_lint, lychee (link checking)
- Security: detect_private_key
- Git: check_merge_conflict, check_case_conflict, no_commit_to_branch
- Files: trailing_whitespace, newlines, mixed_line_ending, fix_smart_quotes
- Meta: check_conventional_commit, mise (config validation), tombi

Mise integration:
- `hk init --mise` — generates hk.pkl AND mise.toml with hk configured
- `hk install --mise` — hooks use `mise x` for execution
- `HK_MISE=1` env var enables mise integration by default
- Tools managed via mise, hooks managed via hk — clean separation

Migration from pre-commit:
- `hk migrate pre-commit` — reads .pre-commit-config.yaml, generates hk.pkl,
  converts repos to builtins where possible. Preserves hooks, patterns,
  and excludes.

Profiles:
- Steps can have `profiles = List("slow")` to only run in specific contexts
- `HK_PROFILE=slow hk check` or `hk check --slow`
- Useful for separating fast pre-commit checks from slow CI checks

Hook types: pre-commit, pre-push, prepare-commit-msg, commit-msg
Plus `hk check` and `hk fix` as standalone commands (not tied to git hooks).

Impact on repo-forge:
This eliminates the entire pre-commit version drift problem from the
aggregation report. No more .pre-commit-config.yaml with stale hook repo
versions. Builtins resolve tool versions through mise. The migration path
exists (`hk migrate pre-commit`) for existing repos.

## What Presets Need to Generate

### Universal (preset:base)

- `mise use hk git-cliff` (resolved live)
- `mise generate bootstrap --localize --write` — contributor onboarding
- `mise generate github-action --write --task=ci`
- `hk init --mise` — generates hk.pkl with builtins + mise.toml integration
- `hk install --mise` — git hooks use mise for tool resolution
- cliff.toml — canonical template, identical across 13 repos
- .gitignore — composed from modules: os-base, security, claude-ai, plus
  language-specific (python, rust, terraform, ansible, docker)
- .editorconfig — standardized indent/line-length rules
- renovate.json — extend shared config + domain presets
- hk.pkl base steps: trailing_whitespace, newlines, check_merge_conflict,
  detect_private_key, check_conventional_commit
- Standard mise tasks: lint (hk check), fix (hk fix), changelog, ci

### Python (preset:uv, expand existing)

Depends on preset:base.

- `mise use python uv ruff git-cliff rumdl` (all resolved live)
- `mise config set env._.python.venv.path .venv`
- `mise config set env._.python.venv.create true -t bool`
- hk.pkl steps: Builtins.ruff, Builtins.mypy, Builtins.markdown_lint,
  Builtins.yamllint, Builtins.shellcheck
- ruff.toml with baseline rules (E, F, I, UP, B, SIM)
- Standard tasks: lint, format, test, check, sync, upgrade
- Hooks: postinstall (hk install --mise, uv sync)

### Rust (preset:rust, new)

Depends on preset:base.

- `mise use rust cargo-audit git-cliff` (resolved live)
- Cargo.toml [lints] section with clippy warnings
- hk.pkl steps: Builtins.cargo_fmt, Builtins.cargo_clippy,
  Builtins.shellcheck, Builtins.taplo (for Cargo.toml)
- Standard tasks: fmt, lint, build, test, audit, ci

### Terraform (preset:terraform, new)

Depends on preset:base.

- `mise use opentofu tflint terraform-docs yamllint`
- hk.pkl steps: Builtins.yamllint, Builtins.shellcheck, custom tflint step,
  custom terraform-fmt step
- .tflint.hcl, .terraform-docs.yml (standardized from existing 3 repos)
- .yamllint.yml (120-char, 2-space)
- Standard tasks: validate, plan, apply per environment

### Ansible (preset:ansible, new)

Depends on preset:base.

- `mise use ansible-core ansible-lint yamllint`
- hk.pkl steps: Builtins.yamllint, Builtins.shellcheck, custom ansible-lint
  step
- .ansible-lint (production profile, Proxmox-aware skip_list)
- .yamllint.yml
- Standard tasks: lint, test, deploy per inventory

## Migration Path for Existing Repos

For the 19 repos currently using pre-commit:
1. `hk migrate pre-commit` — auto-converts .pre-commit-config.yaml to hk.pkl
2. Review generated hk.pkl, replace custom hooks with builtins where possible
3. `hk install --mise` — set up git hooks
4. Remove .pre-commit-config.yaml
5. `mise rm pre-commit` — remove pre-commit from mise tools

This can be a repo-onboard subcommand or a separate migration command.

## Resolved Decisions

- hk replaces pre-commit. Confirmed. 36 releases in 13 months, zero open
  bugs, same author as mise, built-in migration from pre-commit, builtins
  cover the full stack. hclfmt builtin added v1.36.0 for Terraform.
- PKL is the config format for hk. Builtins require it. The `hk init`
  auto-detection (v1.35.0) minimizes the PKL learning curve — most config
  is generated, not hand-written.
- Presets live in ~/.config/mise/tasks/preset/ (global). They scaffold new
  repos that don't have the plugin yet. The mise skill in repo-forge teaches
  how to build, use, and customize presets.
- Version resolution: always `mise use <tool>` for pinning. Never hardcode.
  Default fuzzy pin (e.g., `python = "3.13"`), use `--pin` only when patch
  version matters.

## Unresolved Decisions

- Line length: 88 vs 100 vs 120. Needs a call. 100 is the compromise.
- Ruff rule baseline: E, F, I, UP minimum. B, SIM, PTH for new projects.
- repo-onboard: skill (auto-activates) vs command (explicit /repo-onboard).
  Leaning command — you want to control when scaffolding happens.
- `mise generate bootstrap --localize` vs non-localized as default.

## Next Steps

1. ~~Expand mise skill~~ — DONE. Hooks, presets, `mise generate`, version
   resolution, hk integration all landed with reference files.
   **Cleanup note:** `examples/examples.md` "Common Base Tasks" section still
   references `prek` and `infisical scan`. Update to hk-based tasks once
   preset:base lands.
2. Build preset:base (hk, cliff, gitignore, editorconfig, renovate, bootstrap)
3. Expand preset:uv (add ruff, hk steps, mise hooks)
4. Build repo-onboard command (new repos + migration for existing)
5. Build remaining presets (rust, terraform, ansible) iteratively

-2. Build preset:base (hk, cliff, gitignore, editorconfig, renovate, bootstrap)
+   Installs hk + pkl + git-cliff, writes .gitignore, .editorconfig, cliff.toml,
+   Adds lint/fix/changelog/ci tasks. Tested: hk check, hk fix, pre-commit hook,
+   **Note:** `mise use` writes `"latest"` for tool versions (no `@version` arg).
+   Fine for dev tools — they should float to newest.
3. Expand preset:uv (add ruff, hk steps, mise hooks)
4. Build repo-onboard command (new repos + migration for existing)
5. Build remaining presets (rust, terraform, ansible) iteratively
